text: There is a stark quiet here, illuminated by sterile lights and shadow. The tilted view leaves me disoriented, uncertain if I stand upright or if the world has shifted beneath me. The visible fire hose speaks quietly of preparedness, but the glass walls feel like fragile boundaries. I am acutely aware of my solitude—there is an uncanny, liminal quality to this space. I feel cautious yet curious, standing at the threshold of the unknown.
